mnemonics: Think of a literal fork in a road: one path continues the original rules (soft fork), the other path creates a new, separate road with new rules (hard fork).
keyPoints: A fork is a change to the blockchain's protocol that creates an alternative version of the chain.
A soft fork is backward-compatible; only the updated nodes enforce the new rules.
A hard fork is not backward-compatible and results in a permanent split, creating a new cryptocurrency if there is support.
commonMistakes: Thinking a soft fork also creates a new coin (it does not).
Believing all nodes must upgrade for a soft fork (only miners need to upgrade to enforce new rules).
